Vestigial near-duplicate data source: byte-identical 246,236-row CSV to the '_1' source (different row order, different MD5) with 0 worksheet bindings. Reconciled by modeling ONE physical fact table 'Flight Activity' from the '_1' source (the one all 91 worksheets bind to) and excluding this source entirely, to avoid duplicating 246K rows. Its spatial + table-anchor pseudo-fields are excluded with it.

Extract (.hyper) materialized to CSV and loaded as an Import-mode Power Query partition (Csv.Document, Columns=70, Encoding=65001, explicit en-US Table.TransformColumnTypes to guard against LCID-4096 refresh errors). No live upstream to repoint to; structure-and-numbers demo from the materialized rows.

Spatial geometry field 'Origin Point' = MAKEPOINT(origin_latitude, origin_longitude). Power BI has no native DAX/Power Query geometry type - EXCLUDED from the model. Fallback for pbi-report-builder: feed the raw 'Origin Latitude'/'Origin Longitude' real columns (present on 'Flight Activity') into a custom/AppSource arc/flow-map visual (e.g. Icon Map / Flow Map), or a reduced-fidelity two-point layer.

Spatial geometry field 'Destination Point' = MAKEPOINT(destination_latitude, destination_longitude). No native Power BI equivalent - EXCLUDED. Fallback: raw 'Destination Latitude'/'Destination Longitude' columns on 'Flight Activity' feed a custom arc/flow-map visual.

Spatial geometry field 'Flight Line' = MAKELINE(Origin Point, Destination Point) - the origin->destination route arc. No native Power BI geometry/line equivalent - EXCLUDED. Fallback: a custom arc/flow-map visual bound to the four raw origin/destination lat/long columns, or a two-point (origin,destination) fallback layer. This is the primary map visual of the Flight Page dashboard.

Tableau internal relationship-model table-anchor pseudo-column (data_type 'table', internal_name prefixed [__tableau_internal_object_id__]). Not real data - EXCLUDED from the model entirely (no column, no measure). The vestigial source's equivalent pseudo-column is excluded with that source.

All 8 Tableau live parameters modeled as disconnected single-select slicer tables + a SELECTEDVALUE '<X> Parameter Value' measure defaulting to the Tableau default (Year=2023, Month=7, Region='North America', Airline='SkyConnect Airways', Aircraft Type='A330', Airline Code='GS', Guidelines='Hide Guidelines'). Region 'Global' is the show-all sentinel (Origin Region FILTER bypassed). Not Field Parameters; no relationships (parameter-equality idiom).

Tableau Set parameter holding hundreds of flight-id values; its only consumer was the 'Highlighted Flight Col' field. NOT modeled as a table (would be a large disconnected list). Recommended native equivalent: a slicer on 'Flight Activity'[Flight Id] with report cross-highlighting.

limitations_encountered.item: fields: Selected Flight ID, Selected Flight ID (F), Selected Flight Date, Selected Flight Date (F)
limitations_encountered.issue: Parameter-equality filter-idiom dimensions (IF region/airline/aircraft params match THEN [Flight Id]/[Date] END, used only as exclude-null filters). SIMPLIFIED AWAY - not modeled as calc columns. Report should use native slicers on Airline/Aircraft/Region + the base completed-flight filter instead.

Tableau Set-driven row highlight (IF [Flight Id Set Parameter] = [Selected Flight ID] THEN 1 ELSE 0). Depends on the Flight Id Set + Selected Flight ID (both simplified away). OMITTED as a measure/column - replace with native cross-highlighting or a Flight Id slicer in the report.

Parameter-equality dimension projection (returns Destination City only for rows in the CM month + selected destination region). Replaced with the raw 'Destination City' column + a convenience measure [CM Comp Flights to Destination] (DISTINCTCOUNT of completed flights, CM period, destination-region parameter) so a 'Top Destinations' visual can rank + exclude blanks natively.

SOURCE-WORKBOOK QUIRK preserved faithfully: denominator is [CM No of Comp Flights] (current month), not PM. DAX = DIVIDE([PM No of Mishandled Baggage],[CM No of Comp Flights]). Likely a Tableau authoring bug; flagged for the customer rather than silently 'fixed'.

SOURCE-WORKBOOK QUIRK preserved faithfully: the 'Costs' YoY trend indicator actually compares CY vs PY Op Profit (not Costs). DAX mirrors IF([CY Op Profit]>[PY Op Profit]...). Flagged for the customer.
Informational: the on-time YoY trend indicator compares raw CY vs PY on-time flight COUNT ([CY/PY Count On-Time Perf]), not the on-time percentage. Faithful to source.

No Date table existed in the Tableau workbook (period logic used the fact's year/month integer columns vs parameters). Synthesized a Power Query calendar (2021-2026, buffering the 2022..2025-01 fact domain) related to 'Flight Activity'[Date] for star-schema hygiene and to host the Tableau month/year layout-helper calcs (Year Label, Month List, Label, Row Pos, Col Pos). DataCategory=Time is set in TMDL; formal 'Mark as Date Table' needs live Desktop. KPI measures still filter on the fact's own Year/Month columns (parameter-comparison faithfulness), independent of this relationship.

limitations_encountered.item: fields: Row Pos, Col Pos, Month List, Label, Year Label
limitations_encountered.issue: Row-level month/year layout-helper calcs (small-multiple grid positioning) relocated from the 246K-row fact onto the Date dimension (deterministic from the calendar date), reducing fact width. 'Label' duplicates 'Month List' (first letter of month name) - the Tableau IF was a no-op; both kept for report-binding parity.

limitations_encountered.item: fields: Year Colour, Colour, Bar Colours, Airline Bar Colours
limitations_encountered.issue: Parameter-dependent color-encoding helpers translated to DAX measures (SELECTEDVALUE of Date[Year]/[Month Number]/[Month Start] and Airline vs the parameter values). Assume the consuming visual is grouped by the corresponding Date grain (+ airline filter for 'Airline Bar Colours'); pbi-report-builder may need to adjust the grain reference to match each visual.

Great-circle FLIGHT ROUTE ARC (Tableau MAKELINE/MAKEPOINT geometry, e.g. New York->Dubai) has no native Power BI equivalent. A native Azure Map path/line layer CAN draw arcs but requires PathID+PointOrder wells fed by one data row PER ENDPOINT (path-shaped data). The frozen fact 'Flight Activity' stores Origin+Destination Latitude/Longitude as 4 columns on a SINGLE flight-leg row, which cannot feed the path layer without reshaping the model (forbidden - model is frozen). RESOLUTION: rendered Destination cities as bubbles (Avg Destination Latitude/Longitude by Destination City) on a satellite_road_labels Azure Map + on-canvas fidelity note. RESIDUAL GAP: no drawn arc between origin and destination; the point-to-point connector line is not shown.

limitations_encountered.item: ws.recent_flights + ws.flight list / dashboards[3] Flight Page
limitations_encountered.issue: Tableau's timeline-card flight layout (origin->destination connector dots per flight) simplified to a flat tableEx list (Date/Flight Id/Origin City/Destination City/Aircraft Type/Average Fare Usd/Passengers Carried/Flight Duration Hours), sorted Date desc, filtered by the Airline+Region real-column slicers. Interactive per-flight selection that drives the map in Tableau is not reproduced (no cross-visual selection wired).

2x3 aircraft gallery tiles use PLACEHOLDER images (per-aircraft photo assets are not present in the workbook/repo). 'Details' buttons are decorative shapes; the Tableau interactive drill from a gallery tile into the detail panel is not reproduced.

Right-hand aircraft detail panel is pinned to Aircraft Type='A330' via a visual-level filterConfig (matches the reference screenshot, which shows A330 and has no aircraft-type slicer). Tableau's dynamic selection (click a gallery Details button -> panel updates) is not reproduced because it relied on a Tableau dashboard action; the panel is effectively static to A330.

Load Factor is a double base column with NO formatString in the model. Displayed as a percentage via a projection-level 'format':'0.00%' on the card projection. This per-visual format override is the assumed PBIR mechanism but could NOT be schema-validated offline (schema URL 404s) nor Desktop-verified (bridge busy with concurrent builds). RISK: if the projection 'format' is ignored by Desktop, Load Factor renders as a raw fraction (e.g. 0.79) instead of 78.62%. VERIFY IN DESKTOP.

Fuel Cost KPI uses labelDisplayUnits='1000000000'+labelPrecision to show e.g. '2.92B', but the currency '$' prefix is not rendered (card label text reads '($)'). Minor cosmetic gap; value/magnitude are correct.

The 'vs Prev. Month' delta uses the model's Pos MoM family which is positive-only by construction (negative month-over-month deltas return blank). When the real delta is negative the card shows blank rather than a red down value. Preserved as-is per instruction not to 'fix' source quirks.

Horizontal per-airline breakdown bars render single-color. Tableau colored them by an Up/Down helper; the model's '<metric> Circle Col' helper measures return STRING values (indicator glyph/color names) that cannot drive a PBIR data-color rule directly, so a static bar color is used.

Three Airline summary tiles seen in the reference (Avg Distance Covered, Airline Hub, Major Flight Route) have NO backing measure or resolvable column in the frozen model, so they are rendered as static placeholder cards. Building them would require model changes (forbidden).

Fleet gallery + detail KPIs are base-column aggregations (Sum/Avg) that respond to the Airline+Region REAL-column slicers (defaults Airline='SkyConnect Airways', Region='North America'). If the original Tableau view was fleet-wide (ignoring airline/region), absolute values differ, though gallery and detail stay internally consistent (both are Sum by Aircraft Type).

Live Power BI Desktop screenshot verification is DEFERRED / ENVIRONMENT-CONTENDED. The machine ran multiple concurrent Desktop instances + Mashup containers (parallel sibling builds + user), producing Mashup 'Could not find a PackageSession' refresh errors, and the Desktop Bridge cannot trigger a data refresh headlessly (confirmed by sibling builds). Success is therefore based on STRUCTURAL validation only: (a) powerbi-report-author validate = 0 errors (sole warning PBIR_SCHEMA_UNREACHABLE, an offline schema-fetch artifact), (b) an independent field-reference cross-check of EVERY visual binding against the AirlineAllianceActivity.SemanticModel TMDL display names = 196 references / 66 distinct (table,field) bindings, 0 bad, (c) 0 content overlaps across all 4 pages, (d) skeleton gestalt matches vs all 4 reference screenshots. Items an in-Desktop eye would still confirm (documented, not blocking): Load Factor % projection format, implicit-aggregation cards over summarizeBy:none columns, tableEx flat-list row population, Azure Map render with Avg lat/long.
